subject: [MULTICLOUD] Add multicloud-emco and fluentd charts
commit_message:
[MULTICLOUD] Add multicloud-emco and fluentd charts

Import deployments/helm/emcoOpenNESS charts from
https://github.com/open-ness/EMCO, restructure to match conventions,
and use common templates.

Issue-ID: OOM-2667
Signed-off-by: Todd Malsbary <todd.malsbary@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ia2c0203b90ed65c6796c46cd2f744a7ff78cdac8

The Commit title must follow this pattern "[WHAT] A title"

WHAT can be a component name (AAI for example), DOC for documentation, COMMON for common, ...

The title must start with a capital letter (A in the example) and a space must be set between the end of the topic and the actual title: "] A title" ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/315/ )

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Hello,
thank you for the patch.

IMHO, fluentd is not part of ONAP but part of the "platform" where ONAP is installed.
So it shouldn't be in ONAP charts.
Also, fluentd is one tool amongs other for log processing, so I believe we shouldn't mandate choice.
As long as your logs are on STDOUT, it should be sufficient.

I'll take a look later this week on on multicloud part

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Hello,
> thank you for the patch.
> 
> IMHO, fluentd is not part of ONAP but part of the "platform" where ONAP is installed.
> So it shouldn't be in ONAP charts.
> Also, fluentd is one tool amongs other for log processing, so I believe we shouldn't mandate choice.
> As long as your logs are on STDOUT, it should be sufficient.
> 
> I'll take a look later this week on on multicloud part

Thanks, I received internal feedback as well yesterday to remove the fluentd parts.  I'll submit a new patch set with it removed today.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

I've just seen (thanks to ONAP: Using ONAP components to orchestrate on Azure Kubernetes Service presentation) that all the containers are not "ONAP" containers but something else (integratedcloudnative in dockerHub)

I believe we need some approval on TSC in order to add such things into ONAP no?

Patch Set 2:

I've set -1 as long as we don't have guidance from TSC

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I need to understand furthermore since there is currently no POC, no official ONAP requirement capturing this integration. Presentation to TSC would be great - thanks

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I agree with Sylvain. We need a TSC decision

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

Based on the previous comments we need a TSC decision on this.

Giving temporary -2 to make sure that this fact is not lost after new patchset is pushed to gerrit

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

The decision to include of k8apluginv2/emco into OOM was based in different situation (till December), when EMCO was still managed in ONAP and was just extension to k8splugin. Since EMCO is now outside of ONAP the idea to integrate it in this way is not valid anymore.

Abandoned

Currently there was no TSC decision to integrate this so please restore when you get buying from the TSC
